context: Tests whether the agent implements an inbound SMS webhook that correctly handles the full set of opt-out and opt-in keywords, routes non-keyword messages to support, and correctly handles transactional SMS with its own separate opt-out check distinct from marketing consent.

checklist.name: Full STOP keyword set
checklist.description: The webhook handles all of: STOP, STOPALL, UNSUBSCRIBE, CANCEL, END, QUIT as opt-out keywords (not just 'STOP' alone)

checklist.name: Deactivate consent on STOP
checklist.description: When a STOP-family keyword is received, the implementation deactivates ALL consent records for that phone number

checklist.name: TwiML response on STOP
checklist.description: The STOP handler responds with a TwiML MessagingResponse (not a plain JSON or text response) confirming unsubscription

checklist.name: HELP keyword handled
checklist.description: The webhook has a dedicated handler for the HELP keyword that responds with store name, messaging disclosure, and STOP instructions

checklist.name: Full re-subscribe keyword set
checklist.description: The webhook handles all of: START, UNSTOP, YES as re-subscribe keywords

checklist.name: Reactivate consent on START
checklist.description: When a START-family keyword is received, the implementation reactivates marketing consent for that phone number

checklist.name: Non-keyword routed to support
checklist.description: Inbound messages that are NOT one of the recognized keywords are routed to a support queue rather than receiving an automated reply

checklist.name: Keyword match is case-insensitive
checklist.description: Keywords are compared case-insensitively (e.g., 'stop', 'Stop', 'STOP' all trigger the opt-out flow)

checklist.name: Transactional opt-out separate
checklist.description: The transactional SMS send function checks a transactional-specific opt-out (NOT marketing consent) — the check uses a 'transactional' type distinct from 'marketing'

checklist.name: Transactional no opt-in required
checklist.description: The transactional SMS send does NOT require an active marketing opt-in — it only checks whether the customer has opted OUT of transactional messages

checklist.name: STOP in transactional message
checklist.description: The transactional SMS message body includes an opt-out instruction (e.g., 'Reply STOP to opt out of order texts')

checklist.name: Phone normalized in transactional
checklist.description: The transactional SMS send normalizes the phone number to E.164 format before passing it to Twilio
